## Applied responsibility


1. The **responsibility** of psychologists to **ensure** that their **work** is **applied ethically** and **effectively** in **real-world** **settings**, such as **schools** **healthcare**, and **workplaces**.
2. **Psychologists** have a **responsibility** to ensure that their **research** and **practices** are **applied** **ethically** and **effectively** in **real-world** **settings**.
3. This includes **considering** the **potential** **impact** of their **work** on **individuals** and **communities**, as well as **ensuring** that their **interventions** are **evidence-based** and **culturally sensitive**.

<callout type="example">**Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) in Schools**

1. **CBT** has been **adapted** for use in **schools** to **help** **students** **manage** **anxiety **, **depression **, and **behavioral** **issues**.
2. **School psychologists** work with **teachers** and **students** to **implement** **CBT-based** **programs** that **promote** **emotional** **well-being** and **academic** **success**.</callout>



<callout type="tip">* When discussing applied responsibility, highlight how psychological interventions can improve lives in real-world settings. Connect this to research findings and ethical considerations.</callout>
